channel.title: Claude Guides Blog
channel.description: Practical guides, field reports, and research on Claude Code, AI agents, prompt engineering, and automation.

item.title: Save Claude Tokens by Integrating NotebookLM
item.description: Cut Claude token spend by offloading research and retrieval to Google's NotebookLM — a practical integration pattern with real numbers.

item.title: Hermes: Build a Self-Learning Agent with 7 Workflows
item.description: A Claude Code agent that learns from its own runs — seven composable workflows, full walkthrough.

item.title: Claude Managed Agents: A Practical Playbook
item.description: Patterns, traps, and the playbook we wish existed when we shipped managed agents to production.

item.title: The Complete Claude Tools Guide
item.description: The four levels of Claude tool design — from your first tool to MCP and multi-agent orchestration.

item.title: The Complete Prompt Engineering Guide for Claude
item.description: Every prompt engineering technique that moved quality, ranked by how much it actually helped.

item.title: 5 Non-Code Pipelines You Can Sell Using Claude Code
item.description: Five productized Claude Code pipelines that don't involve writing a single line of application code — and how to price them.

item.title: The 6-Month Roadmap to Becoming an AI Automation Builder
item.description: A concrete month-by-month path from zero to your first paying clients as an AI automation builder.

item.title: 15 Under-Used Claude Code Features (from Boris Cherny)
item.description: Fifteen Claude Code features most users still miss — condensed into a practical checklist.

item.title: 2 Months with Claude Code: A Field Report
item.description: Honest field notes from two months of heavy Claude Code use — what worked, what wasted time, what surprised us.

item.title: Build a Karpathy-Style LLM Knowledge Base in Obsidian
item.description: A step-by-step guide to building a Karpathy-inspired LLM knowledge base in Obsidian and feeding it to Claude.

item.title: AI-First Companies: Restructuring Around Harness Engineering
item.description: What CREAO-style AI-first restructuring means, and why harness engineering is replacing traditional application design.

item.title: The AI Perception Gap: What 632 Users Actually Believe
item.description: A survey of 632 practitioners reveals the gap between what modern AI can do and what people think is possible.
